# Tech stack
- Html, Css, javascript


# Game

Purpose: A child can learn where the letters is on the keyboard. By looking at the Letter shape on the screen, they will look for a similar shape on the keyboar

Core Gameplay Loop
The application follows a simple, high-feedback loop to encourage persistence and pattern recognition:

Visual Prompt: A single, large-format letter appears on the screen.

The Search: the child scans the physical keyboard to find the matching "shape."

The Input:

Success (Correct Key): The screen displays a "Great!" message with positive visual feedback. A cheerful voice-over confirms the success before moving to the next letter.

Failure (Incorrect Key): A neutral "Try again" symbol appears, accompanied by a gentle verbal prompt to encourage another attempt. The letter remains on the screen until the correct key is found.

Suggested Enhancements
To keep the child engaged, consider adding these features in the next iteration:

Case Matching: Toggle between uppercase and lowercase to match the specific printing on your physical keyboard.

Color Coding: Use colors to group "vowels" vs "consonants" to help with visual scanning.

Progress Rewards: After 5 successful matches, play a short animation or show a "Sticker" on the screen.
